title: senecio neomexicanus var. metcalfei
intro: Senecio neomexicanus var. metcalfei, also known as Metcalf's ragwort, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's root and leaves have been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and diuretic properties. Herbal preparations of Senecio neomexicanus var. metcalfei can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in some traditional remedies to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
